name: Two-population (Goldie-Coldman) resistance model — NSCLC first line
description: Mechanistic resistance: a drug-sensitive clone S and a pre-existing drug-resistant clone R, observed together as tumor size V = S + R. The drug kills only the sensitive clone (dS/dt = (kg - kd*E)*S) while the resistant clone grows unchecked (dR/dt = kgr*R), so regrowth after nadir is the resistant subpopulation outgrowing — not a fading drug effect. The kill potency kd is matched to the Claret model so the contrast isolates the resistance MECHANISM. Parameter values are illustrative (NSCLC first-line scale) and unverified; R0 (the initial resistant burden) is biologically interpretable but, like the phenomenological lambda, poorly identified from a short trial.

extraction.source_locator: model form (Goldie-Coldman 1979); value illustrative (matched to Claret kL)
extraction.tier_rationale: Established two-population model form; growth rate is indication-specific.

parameters.label: drug-induced kill potency on the sensitive clone

extraction.source_locator: model form; value illustrative (matched to Claret kD)
extraction.tier_rationale: Kill potency poorly identified from short trials; high IIV CV.
Resistant-clone kinetics rarely measured directly; single-context value.

parameters.label: initial resistant-clone burden (pre-existing resistant fraction x baseline)

extraction.source_locator: model form; value illustrative (~1.5% of a 100 mm baseline)
extraction.tier_rationale: The resistant fraction is biologically interpretable but practically unidentifiable from a short trial; ~100%+ CV is expected.

known_failure_modes.condition: applied to a regimen whose regrowth is not driven by a resistant subclone
known_failure_modes.behavior: R0 / kgr unidentifiable; the resistant tail is fabricated rather than fit
